_comment: Telemetry + process-quality configuration. Copy to quality/telemetry.config.json in the target project.
Keys starting with '_' are documentation and are ignored by all scripts.

telemetry._comment: FACTORY_TELEMETRY=off disables ledger EMISSION only. Honesty checks (vacuity flip, acceptance join, stub scan) live outside this kill switch by invariant A — they cannot be configured off here.

_comment: Verification-class token -> artifact resolution table for check-acceptance-methods --mode=artifact.

Tokens are the closed vocabulary from docs/requirements.template.md. Each declared method must resolve to a fresh, threshold-passing artifact — a declared method with no mechanism is the pixel-perfect failure class.
Per-CLASS, per-PHASE-BAND warning budgets for check-process-ratchet. NEVER a raw total:
in the pixel-perfect run 71 of 73 warnings were benign pre-Phase-6 growth (missing recordings
before the recording phase); a raw ratchet would red honest progress and train reflexive --update.
Class names = the [class] tags printed by the check scripts' WARN lines (warningsByClass in the ledger).
Band keys: 'a-b' (phases a..b inclusive), 'a+' (phase a and later), 'a' (exactly phase a).
If the current phase cannot be parsed from docs/current-state.md, the STRICTEST matching band applies.
'*' is the default budget for classes without their own entry. Budgets can only be tightened once
baselined — check-process-ratchet --update rejects widening writes (tighten-only direction guard).
